statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{b-1}-2}}{{\frac{1}{b-1}+\frac{1}{b-3}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{b-1}-2} and {\frac{1}{b-1}+\frac{1}{b-3}}, which is {\left(b-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(b-3\right)}.
Since b-1 and b-3 were canceled, b \ne 1 and b \ne 3 are in the lost domain condition.
